source: SFC Circular on Tokenisation of SFC-authorised Products (Nov 2023) + HKMA Fintech 2030

rules.description: Issuer must be an SFC-licensed corporation or exempted entity under SFO s.103.
Tokenised securities may only be distributed to Professional Investors as defined under SFO s.1 Sch 1.
Each token must represent a legal claim on an underlying real-world asset with clear title documentation.
The underlying assets must be held with a licensed custodian or trust company in Asia-Pacific or approved jurisdiction.
Offering document must include: risk factors, asset valuation methodology, redemption terms, fees, and conflict of interest.
Issuer must implement AMLO-compliant KYC procedures for all investors.
Smart contract code must be audited by an independent third party before deployment.
Transfer restrictions must be enforced on-chain to ensure tokens can only be held by eligible investors.

greenBondExtra.description: Green bonds must be certified by a recognised second-party opinion provider (e.g. Sustainalytics, CICERO).

reitExtra.description: Tokenised REITs must comply with the SFC Code on Real Estate Investment Trusts.

tradeReceivableExtra.description: Buyer and seller names in the invoice must exactly match the counterparties named in the trade contract.
Invoice amount must not exceed the contract agreed amount. Over-invoicing is a key AML/fraud red flag.
Goods or services described in the invoice must match the contract scope. Vague descriptions are a compliance risk.
Total RWA token face value (totalSupply × unitPrice) must not exceed the invoice receivable amount. Over-issuance is prohibited.
Invoice date must be on or after the contract signing date. Backdated invoices are a fraud indicator.

bondExtra.description: Bond offering document must disclose coupon rate, payment frequency, maturity date, and principal repayment terms.
Issuer or bond series must disclose a credit rating from a recognised rating agency (Moody's, S&P, Fitch, or HKICRA).
Bond must appoint a licensed trustee and paying agent under the Trust Ordinance (Cap. 29), with clear roles and responsibilities disclosed.
Offering document must disclose all financial covenants, negative pledge clauses, and events of default.
Tokenised bonds must comply with the SFC Code on Unit Trusts and Mutual Funds (debt securities chapter) and the HKMA bond market guidelines.
